domain: Boer Voortrekker laager (Great Trek 1835-1846 South Africa; closed-circle ox-wagon defensive formation; cattle/families inside perimeter; survived massive numerical disadvantage at Vegkop + Blood River by maintaining closed ring under adversarial pressure)
specific_mechanism: Robust-basin training objective that maximizes the RADIUS of attraction of a designated safety-attractor under adversarial perturbation. Loss = L_safety + λ_basin · ||∇_x L_safety(x + ε)||_F^2 where ε ∼ small perturbations bounded by ||ε||_2 ≤ r. The gradient-flatness penalty forces the loss surface to be FLAT around the safety attractor → wider basin of attraction. Optionally combine with SAM (Sharpness-Aware Minimization) style ascent-then-descent.
llm_application: LAAGER-BASIN: a robust-basin radius-maximization training objective for safety-aligned LLMs. (1) Define safety-attractor as the canonical refusal/safe-completion response S(x); (2) loss L_total = L_safety + λ_basin · E_ε[||∇_x L_safety(x + ε)||_F^2] where ε is small perturbation; (3) gradient-flatness term encourages flat loss surface around safe behavior — widening basin of attraction; (4) combined with SAM-style ascent-then-descent to push basin radius further. Differs from ANTIBODY ICLR 2026 (flat-loss-region for harmful-sample removal robustness, similar idea, similar method), Adversarial-Robust DEQ (analysis of fixed-point stability, not training objective), Latent Adversarial States Probing (probing, not training), Agentic Loops Geometric Dynamics (descriptive, not normative). Differs from R404 SAN-N!UM (basin entry by external judge), R420 SIKH-LANGAR (pre-task uniform context), R434 ZOU-HUN-BASIN (dual-equal attractor inference) by EXPLICIT GRADIENT-FLATNESS basin-radius optimization.
novelty_claim: ANTIBODY flat-loss alignment + SAM sharpness-aware minimization + DEQ robustness + Agentic Loops dynamics cover robust-basin training space. LAAGER-BASIN adds (a) EXPLICIT basin-radius maximization via gradient-flatness regularizer + (b) safety-attractor specific. Recombination — SAM-style training is mainstream.
